reviews.review_text:
Nice park to stay
Amenities are very clean
clean
Reasonable prices
Reception lady very nice and accomodating

Writing
To Whom It May Concern,
Subject   Positive
I am writing this letter to formally express my sincere appreciation and recognition for the outstanding service, professionalism, and welcoming environment provided by the management and staff at River Retreat Caravan Park in Tweed Heads South.
As a current resident of the park, my family and I have had the opportunity to experience first-hand the exceptional level of care and dedication demonstrated by both the owners and their team. It is not often that accommodation providers maintain such a high standard of hospitality, organisation, and genuine concern for the wellbeing of their residents and guests.
From the moment we arrived, we were treated with respect, kindness, and professionalism. The management team operates the park with clear attention to detail, fairness, and a strong commitment to maintaining a safe and pleasant environment for everyone staying here. The staff members consistently go above and beyond in their roles, ensuring that residents and visitors feel welcome, supported, and comfortable at all times.
One of the most notable aspects of River Retreat Caravan Park is the way the property is managed and maintained. The grounds are well kept, facilities are clean and functional, and there is an obvious pride taken in maintaining the park to a very high standard. This level of upkeep reflects the dedication of the owners and staff to providing a quality living and holiday environment for families and long-term residents alike.
For families in particular, the park offers a relaxed and secure atmosphere that makes it an ideal place to stay. It is clear that the management places a strong emphasis on community, safety, and respectful living. This creates a positive environment where people can enjoy their time without unnecessary stress or disruption.
I would also like to commend the professionalism shown by the management in their day-to-day operations. Any concerns raised are handled promptly and respectfully, and communication with residents is clear and fair. These qualities are essential in any residential park environment and they are consistently demonstrated here.
It is for these reasons that I am very pleased to provide this formal written acknowledgement of the excellent service and management of River Retreat Caravan Park. In my opinion, this park represents one of the best examples of how a caravan park should be operated — with integrity, professionalism, and genuine care for the people who live and stay here.
My family and I are extremely grateful for the positive experience we have had while residing at the park. It has proven to be a comfortable, welcoming, and well-run place that we would confidently recommend to other families, travellers, and residents looking for a safe and enjoyable place to stay.
Based on our personal experience, I would not hesitate to rate River Retreat Caravan Park as a five-star establishment, and in many respects it exceeds that standard. The commitment shown by the owners and staff deserves recognition and appreciation.
Please accept this letter as a formal statement of commendation and support for the management and staff of River Retreat Caravan Park. Their professionalism and dedication make a significant difference to the experience of everyone who stays here.
Thank you for maintaining such a wonderful place for families and residents.
Yours sincerely,
David Thomas
Great place visit and live

Was stalked and nearly murdered by an occupant of their park, contacted police. Asked park to refund me the days I hadn't used so I can relocate due to their error. I was denied refund, but the attempted murderer was refunded for her stay when asked to move on by park owners.

The dangerous individual followed me to the park after being departed, asked for a refund again to no avail. Would not recommend this park to anyone ESPECIALLY IF YOU HAVE KIDS.

Wouldn’t recommend this park. 👎                                                                          We arrive 12:35 didn't realise that check-in was 1 o'clock. We had been traveling Since 6am and on arrival, both myself and my wife were both busting to go to the toilet. We were told we weren't allowed to use the toilets until we checked in. Finally checked in at 1 o'clock and were told the rules about our little Frenchie who has been travelling with us you wouldn't even know we had a dog that's how well-behaved he is. We have eyes in this park so keep your dog on a lead at all times. I found them to be very rude and abrupt, finally got our sight and it was a patch of
dirt and we had to set up our swag on it for the night.
The other two sites that were next to us  both had nice green grass  and no one set up on those sites over night.
I'm happy it was only one night stay before we moved on.
The cleaner Should not turn the shower head up the wrong way
As I wasn't very happy to get all my stuff wet.
We found this was the worst caravan park we stayed at on our 28 day road trip.

We stayed here for one night between Christmas and NYE at an unpowered site. The staff were very lovely. Pool, bbq area and bathrooms were extremely clean. Quiet. Right on the water. Great place to stay for a night or two while passing through.
Great place to stay near the water clean and tidy

We have used a powered Site which were roomy and level. Even though there are a lot of permanent it was quiet and well maintained. The staff were so helpful and friendly and the amenities were lovely and clean. You do have a few jets flying over to the airport but you soon get use to the sound and it’s only really in daytime hours and a few in the evening.
Thank you Di for making our stay a pleasant and memorable one.

Great caravan park near Tweed River. Noisy with planes flying quite low over the park.
Clean amenities and caravan sites okay too.
Unfortunately the park doesn't have a cancellation policy even with ample notice but retain the full amount for the next visit. Didn't want to do that as during COVID lockdown we lost our deposit as apparently managers/ booking system changed.Lots of long term residents which makes the park less touristy. Dog friendly a plus

We booked in for a week stay for the school holidays and would definitely recommend. Nice and quiet park with a beautiful bali style pool that has a river view. Absolutely stunning. Powered sites are big with concrete slabs. Staff are super friendly, amenities are clean and well looked after. Huge clotheslines & a laundry as well. River straight across the road for fishing which the kids loved! I don't understand the bad reviews! It's been fabulous.

Our powered site looked on to the river next to the pool. Really enjoyed our stay, helpful staff, amenities clean and spacious. Yes the park is under the flight path to airport but it was fine not unbearable. We would stay again. Our dog loved all the grass. No problems

Fantastic quiet park
Staff exceptional in service and friendliness
Spotless showers laundry and good big showers with pressure!
Huge sites !
Pet friendly ! Great camp kitchen !
Definately will return
Thanks Marty and Di !!

Unfortunately it wasn’t what we expected according to website. Lots of permanents living and it’s under the airport flight path. At the time they were doing extensive stormwater drainage which was noisy

Basil Faulty has nothing on the managers of River Retreat Caravan Park, be aware that there is no customer focus or service, the grass areas are still suffering from ground water flooding and wheel tracks everywhere on the grass sites, dug up dirt from previous people trying to get caravans out of the narrow sites, some attempt with bark and wood chip to pack up the ground was a significant failed attempt to hide all the flooding damage but still full prices for underrated sites where being asked, do yourself a favour and bypass this poorly managed caravan park.

Inadequate WiFi signal strength leaves even cabins close to reception without functional internet (whether for usage on own devices, or for cabin's television's internet functionality [YouTube, Netflix, etc.]). ||Were given one towel for the cabin to last a three-night stay. When we asked reception for some extras, the receptionist objected pissily, as if we were asking the world of her; apparently the expectation was that we would wash and dry the single towel in the park's laundry facility after each use, at $4 per load in the drier.||Not disclosed in any terms and conditions available to peruse prior to booking, the cabin features lovely laminated signs stuck to the walls informing guests that a $25 fine will be charged if the bins in the cabin are not emptied by the guest prior to their departure, or if any spoons or cups are left in or beside the sink. Because god forbid that the price of the cabin already include basic cleaning-between-guests that every single other accommodation-provider everywhere performs without making an issue of it. Left a muffin wrapper in the bin did you?! How dare you impose the burden of cleaning up after you on us, after paying us hundreds to stay here!||Hot water tank/system for the shower seems to hold enough hot water for exactly half of a quick shower. Lovely.||On our final evening, got to enjoy the late night entertainment of hearing a man running through the site screaming for a pursuer to get away from him. Was either some bloke being chased by a knife-wielding assailant, or just a druggie hallucinating. Either way, at least it was interesting.||Otherwise, the cabin was serviceable.||As far as the hospitality of the receptionists, the thin older receptionist was lovely, while the chubby blonde towel-hoarder needs to work on her manners.

River Retreat was great.  Very clean, friendly, quiet.  Mixture of long term residents and holiday makers.
Put 5 stars for room but we were on a site which was perfect.  Lots of room.  Close to the river and all amenities.
Will definitely be going here again.

We stayed here recently for a one-night stopover and found the experience to be fairly average overall.

On the positive side, they did cater for after-hours check-in, which was appreciated and made our arrival smooth and convenient.

That said, the tourist van sites had several drawbacks. There were large tree stumps scattered throughout the available sites, and a visible pile of garden waste and rubble at the back, which detracted from the overall presentation. Our site was located under a large, older gum tree — which made us feel a little uneasy after seeing a sign near the toilet block warning guests to “watch for falling branches.”

The sites themselves were quite long, which could suit some setups, but for us it meant being too far away from power, water, and grey water points — our hoses and cables simply couldn’t reach.

The amenities (toilets and showers) seemed to be in reasonable condition, with no issues during our brief stay.

A major downside was the constant noise from overhead aircraft — it honestly felt like we were just off the end of a runway, and the noise was hard to ignore.

Lastly, it’s worth noting the park appears to have a significant number of long-term residents, giving it a different feel compared to your typical tourist-focused holiday park.

It was okay for an overnight stop, and the after-hours check-in was a plus, but I’d be hesitant to stay longer without some improvements being made to the tourist site area.
Straight under flight path from airport

Beware - both this park's website page and wikicamps listing states they are dog friendly. This is not the case. We were informed today that they no longer accept pets. They sent us to a park down the road instead who also did not accept pets (BIG4 - which in their defence state they do not accept pets). Sadly looking at recent reviews it was a blessing in disguise that we were unable to stay.

We stayed at this campsite for 4 nights, and it was by far the worst campsite we have stayed at on our east coast tour so far. We spent most of the time trying not to be at the campsite.

Negatives: Unfriendly, and very rude front of house staff, overflowing bins on our campervan patch, where we had to pick up other people’s rubbish as birds were attacking the exposed bin bags. When I brought this issue up to the front of house staff, their response was, ‘well you’re leaving tomorrow’, which we weren’t and it was besides the point. Unwelcoming, hostile energy from the permanent residents who lived there - on our final morning the police arrived to speak to one of the neighbours, which added to the uncomfortable, negative environment.

Positives: spacious and clean toilet and shower block.

The owner will probably reply to this review and try and explain away what happened, but I really do implore everyone to visit a different campsite.
On website said they have availability, I called and a rude and deaf woman yells at me that she is fully booked. Thank god for that

Sylvia who is the manager told me she couldn't live with herself if she didn't refund me after staying 2 nights and paying for 1 week to be close to my mother who was receiving end of life care in a nearby hospital. Well obviously she can live with herself.
She just kept stringing me along telling me story after story about head office has to send the refund. It obviously is all bull! Even the office lady kept up the bull too. I’m so disappointed in your promises.
Massive sites and great kept utilities with good fishing straight across the road

We arrived to check in and the time was approx 10 mins into their lunch break. Management answered the phone & we were told that we have to come back later. The lady was quite rude and blunt. We then had to reverse the caravan back out of the driveway onto the main road and find somewhere to park for an hour.
WHERE IS THE CUSTOMER SERVICE??? > THERE WAS NONE.
I booked & paid for a large grass site , once we found our site, there was a big dirt patch where the annex was going.
And when we sat down to try and relax ( in between the planes which were quite regular > we did not expect or know that the park is right under the flight path for the Gold Coast airport ), our view from the door of our caravan was a dirty old dilapidated fibro shed.
After the rain,  there was at least 3 inches of water in and around the van. Just about all of the grassed areas were a lake.
NOT VERY PLEASANT - VERY POOR DRAINAGE.
Yes there were nice shady trees, but also dangerous with LARGE overhanging branches.
This also puts a uncomfortable feel about the place.
There are a number of permanent sites and most of the ones we seen were very untidy giving the park a run down feel.

WE WILL NOT RECOMMEND THIS PARK TO ANYONE AND WILL NOT BE RETURNING EVER AGAIN.
Felt watched, being squeezed in. Looked untidy.

Although the location and amenities are great, our stay was spoilt the moment we arrived by the rudest manager we have ever experienced.
Booked site 2.5mths ago, paid in full, rec'd conformation email. BUT then my c/c was charged an extra $7 the day before arriving. When I politely questioned the charge via email, was told it was balance of my deposit, I advised the park via email I already had paid in full and had conformation email stating $50p/n. They agreed to refund making the excuse it was because I booked online. On arrival was greeted with 'Who are you?'.No hello or welcome. Then was given a lecture by woman manager like I was a naughty child. That their sites are definately $57 & that's what "their systems" says. Offered to show her the confirmation email, but she wasn't interested as she knew if had been confirmed incorrectly - their fault not mine. Rambled on about that it was because we had booked online. Then finished with "hope you're good at backing because it's not an easy site" When the male manager suggested we could be move her response was "They booked online, I can't change it!"
Some people should just not be in hospitality or tourism and this female manager definitely fits this category. Will never be back and ward off this park.
The reception lady is so rude

I travel with a spoodle dog. The pet friendly cabin was absolutely fit for purpose. I have noticed a few other comments about pet owners being treated like 2nd class citizens when comparing the pet friendly cabins to the non-pet friendly on the site. Yes, the pet friendly cabins are more Travelodge than the Ritz, but absolutely fit for purpose. Not all pet owners are as considerate as they should be and I am extremely grateful for the opportunity to travel with my pet and stay in such a comfortable bed. At 82 years of age, the alternative would be sitting patiently waiting for God. I have a fat tyre etrike, and the ride to Froggy Beach was safe and enjoyable. The bike path along the beach front from Froggy beach to  Coolongatta, Long Beach, Currumbin was extremely pleasurable. |Sylvia was fantastic. She is a gem.  As is Wendy who took my booking. |Thank you, Sylvia and Wendy, I'll (we'll) be back.
Great Park this is my second time here

The caravan park is so cramped. Small sites making manoeuvring your rig difficult. We arrived on a Sunday , no one was in attendance so they left us written instructions where our site was. Turns out they gave us the wrong instructions where we , because of their stuff up, took someone else’s space.|Not a single rubbish recycling bin. Everything goes into the red rubbish bins. Disgraceful!. Lady at front desk was extremely rude on our departure. On top of all that it’s very noisy with low flying aircraft overhead every 5 to 10 minutes. Never again!

We booked 2 nights on a large site with our dogs. We had an electrical problem with our van. Sylvia and her husband not only arranged an extra night, they found someone to fix our problem. Wonderful people and a very friendly park, very clean as well.

We booked this place weeks in advance, only to be told after waiting out front for 30 minutes at check-in that our site was too small, despite having provided our caravan measurements when we booked. The lady at reception informed us that they were fully booked and couldn’t move us. After making us feel like an inconvenience for about 5 minutes, she managed to place us on a site under large gum trees, which I was not comfortable with due to the large amounts of pollen, bird droppings, and bat droppings. Since we had booked this place for three weeks, I asked if we could be moved to another site, which was denied, even though there were empty sites available. We returned to our van to find that a branch had fallen and put a hole in our new awning. As you could imagine, i was pissed! The lady at reception will not go out of her way to assist you, and if she does, she will make you feel like a massive inconvenience. She is very negative and lacks customer service skills. We ended up leaving early despite paying in advance as they do not offer refunds. The caravan park itself and location are okay.
Die anderen Bewertungen können wir nicht nachvollziehen. Wir wurden freundlich empfangen und auch die anderen Bewohner des Parks waren nett. Wir kommen gerne wieder.
Lot of planes coming and going noisy

I don't understand how this place is still operating. This place is a completely joke! I could write so many bad things about this place starting from the misleading advertisement and so on. This is not a pet friendly park. Be aware and Avoid at any cost!
Great place to stay with friends

Booked via an app. Warm reception and room was neat and tidy. Pity shower head wasted more water than I got to use. I was in first cabin so when people does not turn off their cars at gate the fumes choke you.
And again I was in nice setting but had to leave windows closed because of that and the smokers next door.
Apart from that and the ants I had a good stay
We had an absolute wonderful stay.  The amenities were fantastic and very well maintained.  Straight opposite the river where we did some fishing.  I would definitely recommend this park 😁
Had to cancel our booking and same day received my deposit back!  Great service thank you.

Edited to add - The comment made by the Managers in response to my review is an outright lie. I absolutely did respond to this person 'verbally' but did not use those words- and only after he was overly aggressive and physically intimidating toward myself and my child and. I have witnesses from that caravan park who approached me after this incident. It was a sweltering day, there was no one in the pool and we were respectful of our surroundings. I admit I should not have used the pool without permission but my child and I did not deserve to be physically and verbally threatened.

The view is spectacular but the place is run by a bully!

Quiet, peaceful...great service. Off the beaten track and far enough away that you can really feel like you are escaping. Really lovely walk along the river through mangrove forests. Pet friendly so my dog and I love this place.

The best place we stayed in a 3000km trip. Wendy, the host, was delightful to communicate with. The facilities are brilliantly clean and new, and the location alongside the Tweed River was beautiful. We experienced kookaburra, a friendly large ?bush lizard visiting our site, and a beautiful social ibis during our stay. The pool was a delight to swim in, and we had the best time during our stay here.

I can’t say visiting this park was a great experience.. our children (8&10 years) were constantly watched and intimated not only by the management but also by some residents… we were caravaning with family and friends and basically got read the riot act upon arrival, I felt like our every move was watched.
Apart from a very small pool the part has very little to offer, on a positive note the amenities were very clean and presented well, some great sized caravan sites and tree coverage for shade.
The park is directly under the flight path for the local airport which can also be noisy during the daytime… unfortunately we have no plans on returning to the park..

The management quote to our kids  "This is NOT a fun park" on day one, pretty much says it all, it is lovely location & clean amenities. However the people who are running the place need some people skills. Extremely unfriendly & we felt we were under constant surveillance. 😪 unfortunately wouldn't reccomend or return.
Clean, comfortable cabin. Helpful friendly staff.

A+ on service, facilities and amenities. I booked the budget accommodation for myself and two dogs, I was not expecting much for $100 a night for pet friendly accommodation on tweed at Christmas so it's been a really pleasant surprise. ||The quality of the accommodation is great value for money and the service at the retreat is better than a 5 star hotel! The budget accommodation is clean and tidy, there is A/C and fan, kitchenette, TV, plenty of storage The extra touches have been executed with love including clean crisp white sheets, a nicely made bed, towels neatly presented, Tea and coffee, and shower products provided. ||Wendy has been so lovely and the accommodation is comfortable, safe and clean. So it's disappointing to see that there have been poor reviews on this accommodation- just don't believe everything you read, people are far more likely to write a bad review then take the time to write a good one. ||I felt the need to counter a few of the negative comments- I think they're unfair and keyboard warriors should be more wary of what a bad review (that is not legitimate/justified) does to a small business. Please note that I have no affiliation to Rivers Retreat, I am simply a happy guest. ||1. Pet owners: Getting put in the older accommodation should realise that other visitors without pets may not want to sleep in the same place that your dog has. Be thankful that there is still an affordable place to stay with your pets, or dig into your wallets and pay a lot more to holiday with them.|2. Poor service: I can't understand this comment, my dealings with the retreat have been above and beyond- very welcoming and so helpful. Perhaps the people reviewing were having a bad day...?|3. Accommodation was tired: I assume that the comment is regarding the budget accommodation which an older style (but still very comfortable). Similar to the pet owner comment...You get what you pay for, the retreat offers brand new accommodation as well if you have the $$..||I stumbled across reviews trying to work out what to expect/bring for my stay, so the only thing I would suggest is perhaps more of a description/promotion of the different types of your accommodation for guests (e.g. no ensuite in budget accomm- although I was told over the phone when booking so this was clear for me but perhaps overlooked by online bookings). I have posted my photos (and furry friends) in the pics. But all in all- I would highly recommend River Retreat Caravan Park for your next Tweed holiday, enjoy :)|||

Hmmm.. I don’t generally write reviews however the experience during this trip to this park has prompted me to write one. We have stayed at this park 3 times over the past 15 months for between 2 to 3 weeks each time as it has been convenient for us, as 27 year recent locals, to see friends and old work associates along with it being pet friendly. The reception this visit, due to having a dog, has not been experienced before from the heavy dog rules scrutiny drilled in at check in to being abruptly told the rules again along with a threat of being removed during the stay. On this day my wife and I prepared for a ride on our push bikes with our dog so I removed him from the van lead under the awning to allow him to walk to my wife which was no further than 4mtrs within our site boundaries to be aggressively informed by the female manager the dog rules and the threat of being removed if we didn’t comply. This verbal spray was totally uncalled for as our dog didn’t stray from our boundaries and was sitting waiting to be put into his bike seat. The interaction left me furious especially as I see during early morning walks that a permanent lets their cat roam and another dog escaped their owners control and wandered through the park however the managers drink with the long term and permanent guests! I understand the dog rules as they apply at all pet friendly parks and I also get the unfortunate fact that not all dog owners are responsible which create neighbourhood and park issues however the managers are taking it too far which will inevitably make people with dogs think twice before coming to this park noting that we will not return. The park is very average and there are other better options for guests not liking dogs so if the goal is to become non pets then it will be a mostly empty park other than permanents.
Another example of the unwelcoming and aggressive nature of this female manager was when my daughter called the telephone number of the office when it was unattended around 2pm to inform them that a delivery person was there to drop off a parcel only to receive the following reply when the phone was answered “I’m busy at the moment so I’ll call you back” and then hung up the phone! Really poor management which will do the owners more harm than good over their remaining tenure.

Touring around the Tweed Coast. A very nice Park next to the Tweed River. The sites vary from small, medium to spacious for the bigger vans and RV's. The amenities are kept clean (visitors not so). Quiet at night even though it's close to the Gold Coast Airport, but since being here I've only heard 2 or 3 flights. Staff are very helpful and polite. Close to Tweed Mall and Tweed City Shopping Centres. Short drive to Coolangatta, and some of the best beaches in Australia.

Caravan sites were a good size and not cramped. We will stay there again. Grounds were well maintained.
Good clean amenities.
Great little park ,enjoyed our stay
Comfortable stay
Great location

Love love love , this caravan Park great place to take your caravan. Facilities very clean , across the street is the river,even black Swan's. Close to shopping centres short drive to the gold coast. Staff friendly.
Clean cabins,  close to water

We had a çaravan site. The lady who checked us in was very friendly and explained everything to us. The van park was one of the cleanest I have been to. The amenities were cleaned everyday and the van sites were clean. The bbq area was a good size with tables and chairs to eat at. There was seating around the park and dogs were aloud there also.  Lots of friendly people to talk to. The cabins were also a good size for a family.

Bring earplugs if you want a decent night sleep. The amount of planes flying overhead, particularly early in the morning is ridiculous. I understand that this is not just limited to this park.
Ask for a large site. Our neighbour, who had to u hitch/rehitch to get his van onsite was astounded that my hubby managed this. It doesn't help when you have to scrap against palm tree fronds to manoeuvre.
The rest of the park is ok but the toilet block is a bit dark and dingey during the day.
Stayed there in mid June with our pet no problems or issues with other pets top place to stay ,amenities great ,people who run park friendly and pleasant will stay there again.

We travel with a small dog so always request pet friendly accomodation. We stayed at River Retreat Caravan Park in July 2022 and had a great pet friendly cabin. Have just started in June 2023 and would appear the new owners now rate travellers with pets as 2nd class and put you in the really old cabins. Won't be back for sure.
A bit expansive where can get other caravan parks 4o
